### Mean Time to Resolution

The average time, in minutes, from when an incident is detected to when it is
fully resolved.

*Alternative names*:

- MTTR
- time to resolve
- incident resolution time
- mean time to recovery (see the interpretation note on this overloaded term)

**Calculation.** Average `mttr_detected_resolved` (minutes from `detected_at` to
`resolved_at`, precomputed in the table) across the incidents whose `detected_at`
falls in the reporting window, from `sre.mttr_current`. Because the incident
timestamps are stored as America/Toronto strings rather than native dates, the
window casts the `YYYY-MM-DD` prefix of `detected_at` to a `DATE` instead of
filtering a `from_date` column. Unresolved incidents have a null
`mttr_detected_resolved` and are excluded from the average.

To break the figure out for service-level reporting, group the same average by
`severity`.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

Overall:

```sql
SELECT
    AVG(mttr_detected_resolved) AS mean_time_to_resolution
FROM sre.mttr_current
-- set the reporting window on detected_at; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
WHERE CAST(substr(detected_at, 1, 10) AS DATE) BETWEEN DATE '2026-01-01' AND DATE '2026-06-26';
```

By severity:

```sql
SELECT
    severity,
    AVG(mttr_detected_resolved) AS mean_time_to_resolution
FROM sre.mttr_current
-- set the reporting window on detected_at; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
WHERE CAST(substr(detected_at, 1, 10) AS DATE) BETWEEN DATE '2026-01-01' AND DATE '2026-06-26'
GROUP BY severity
ORDER BY severity;
```

## R

Overall:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("sre", "mttr_current")) |>
  # set the reporting window on detected_at; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
  filter(
    as.Date(str_sub(detected_at, 1, 10)) >= as.Date("2026-01-01"),
    as.Date(str_sub(detected_at, 1, 10)) <= as.Date("2026-06-26")
  ) |>
  summarise(
    mean_time_to_resolution = mean(mttr_detected_resolved, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

By severity:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("sre", "mttr_current")) |>
  # set the reporting window on detected_at; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
  filter(
    as.Date(str_sub(detected_at, 1, 10)) >= as.Date("2026-01-01"),
    as.Date(str_sub(detected_at, 1, 10)) <= as.Date("2026-06-26")
  ) |>
  group_by(severity) |>
  summarise(
    mean_time_to_resolution = mean(mttr_detected_resolved, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  arrange(severity)
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* The figure is in minutes; divide by 60 for hours. It is a
**mean**, so a single very long incident can pull it up sharply, especially given
how few incidents there are in any window. Read it alongside the incident count,
and consider a median or a Sev1-only cut when one outlier dominates.

The window is anchored on `detected_at`, so an incident counts in the period it
*began*, even if it was resolved in a later period. `severity` is unvalidated
free text in the source sheet, so the grouped view reflects whatever values were
typed (expect the occasional typo or spacing drift).
